Design Philosophy

Why emotion
is the interface.

"Design should feel like breathing — invisible yet essential."
🩵
Emotional Clarity
Calm color, thoughtful structure, and hierarchy that guides without forcing. Every element earns its place.
🧭
Behavioral Awareness
Design that respects human rhythm — turning complex behaviors into effortless habits through intentional friction reduction.
🌸
Meaningful Minimalism
Only what feels necessary remains. Visual noise is removed, leaving space for emotion to breathe.
Selected Work

A trilogy of emotion,
behavior, and purpose.

01 / 03
Mood-First UX · Lifestyle

🎧 VIBE — Discover by Emotion, Not Location

"A mood-first lifestyle app that acts like your emotional compass."

Traditional discovery apps show what to do — VIBE shows how it'll feel. Users begin by selecting their current emotional state, and the app curates perfectly matched experiences nearby. Decision fatigue → emotional alignment.

85%
Mood-match success
40%
Faster decisions
5
Emotion states
02 / 03
Emotional Commerce · E-commerce

🛍️ AISLE — Shopping, Simplified Emotionally

"Interest-based e-commerce designed for emotional connection."

Most e-commerce focuses on "more." AISLE does the opposite — curating products around what users genuinely love, replacing clutter with calm discovery. Your profile is your emotional identity, not just your wishlist.

70%
Left apps due to clutter
60%
Shop by mood
03 / 03
Behavioral Design · Healthcare

💊 AnchorDoze — Healing Through Habit

"A behavioral UX intervention for antibiotic adherence."

Medication adherence isn't a logic problem — it's emotional and behavioral. AnchorDoze connects doses to existing daily habits (like morning coffee), making healthcare feel like ritual, not obligation. Behavior change begins with empathy.

85%
Test completion rate
60%
User confidence boost
80%
AMR awareness increase
Design Threads

What connects
all my work.

🩵
Emotion as Logic
Every design decision is rooted in how users feel before they act. Emotion is data, and great interfaces translate it.
🧠
Behavioral Design
Making habits effortless, not forced. I use behavioral frameworks like habit stacking and BJ Fogg's model to drive real change.
🌸
Human Calm
Creating peace in digital chaos. In a world of noise, calm is a competitive advantage — and a design choice.
🎯
Meaningful Aesthetics
Clarity wrapped in emotion. Visual language that doesn't just look good — it communicates trust, warmth, and clarity at a glance.
About Priyanshu

A scientist who
found design as the most human form of problem-solving.

I come from a background in Microbiology and Healthcare Management, which gave me an unusual lens for design — I see human behavior as systems, and I design the interventions.

"I design to make people feel seen — not just served."

Design, to me, is the bridge between science, emotion, and behavior. Every pixel decision is a hypothesis. Every user flow is an experiment in empathy.

Currently exploring design systems for healthcare, product emotion mapping, and ethical AI experiences.
